mappings.rationale: Directly addresses the organization of human work, its social dimensions, and the effects of rationalization on workers.
Covers the application of scientific methods to managerial functions and the organization of production.
Addresses how organizations structure and manage work, including systems engineering and organizational design.
Relevant to systems engineering and the utilization of scientific and technological knowledge in planning and designing complex systems.
Covers the transformation from craft-based to mass production and the use of machines as replacements for labour.
Directly relevant to the section's coverage of the relation between man and machine and automation in industrial production.
Relevant to automation and the changing relationship between humans and machines in production.
Covers research and development processes relevant to systems engineering and operations research.
Marx's theories on labor organization, division of labour, and the effects of industrialization on workers are directly relevant to this section.
Covers the economic systems that shaped changes in production techniques and mass production.
Relevant to the section's coverage of optimization theory and game theory as tools for systems-design techniques.
Directly addresses the management of human labor within organizations, including psychological and social aspects of work.
Relevant to managerial functions and the organization of human work within production systems.
Covers the organization of production ventures and the application of management methods to new enterprises.
Relevant to the psychological and social aspects of mass production and the effects of rationalization of work on mankind.
